title: Cryptography & Information Security: EML Hardness Hierarchy

key_theorem.statement: Perfect secrecy (OTP) is EML-0 operationally (I(M;C)=0). RSA encryption C=M^e mod n is EML-2 (modular exponentiation). Factoring and discrete log are EML-∞ (no polynomial algorithm classically). GNFS is sub-exponential: exp((ln n)^{1/3}·(ln ln n)^{2/3}) = EML-2 (two nested logs). Hash outputs are EML-∞ (no structure, preimage/collision resistance). Birthday bound is EML-2 (2^{n/2} = square root). LWE hardness is EML-∞ (worst-case lattice). Shor's algorithm reduces factoring to EML-2 (quantum polynomial).

otp.reason: OTP: I(M;C)=0 = perfect secrecy = EML-0 cipher; H(K)≥H(M) = EML-2 bound

caesar.reason: C = (M + k) mod 26: affine map over Z_26 = EML-0 (modular arithmetic)
caesar.security: Breakable by frequency analysis — EML-0 offers no computational security

shannon_perfect_secrecy.insight: Perfect secrecy is EML-0 operationally but requires EML-2 key entropy

rsa_example.reason_enc: C = M^e mod n: modular exp = EML-2 (exp of log)
rsa_example.reason_hard: Factoring n to recover d: EML-∞ (best known = GNFS ~ exp((ln n)^{1/3}))

dh_example.reason: g^x mod p: EML-2 to compute, EML-∞ to invert (DLP)

gnfs.reason: GNFS: exp((ln n)^{1/3}·(ln ln n)^{2/3}) = sub-exponential = EML-2 (two nested logs)
GNFS: exp((ln n)^{1/3}·(ln ln n)^{2/3}) = sub-exponential = EML-2 (two nested logs)
GNFS: exp((ln n)^{1/3}·(ln ln n)^{2/3}) = sub-exponential = EML-2 (two nested logs)
GNFS: exp((ln n)^{1/3}·(ln ln n)^{2/3}) = sub-exponential = EML-2 (two nested logs)

public_key.hardness_summary: Cryptographic one-way functions: EML-2 to evaluate, EML-∞ to invert

birthday_bounds.reason: Birthday bound 2^{n/2} = exp(n·ln2/2): EML-2 (exp of linear in n)
Birthday bound 2^{n/2} = exp(n·ln2/2): EML-2 (exp of linear in n)
Birthday bound 2^{n/2} = exp(n·ln2/2): EML-2 (exp of linear in n)
Birthday bound 2^{n/2} = exp(n·ln2/2): EML-2 (exp of linear in n)

merkle_damgard.reason: f(H_{i-1}, m_i) iterated: EML-1 sequence (geometric chain of EML-1 steps)

sha256_avalanche.reason: Avalanche: 1-bit change → ~50% output change = EML-∞ (no structure in output)

lwe.reason_hard: LWE hardness ≡ gap-SVP: worst-case lattice problem = EML-∞
LWE hardness ≡ gap-SVP: worst-case lattice problem = EML-∞

grover.reason: Grover: O(√N) = O(2^{n/2}): quadratic speedup = EML-2 (halves exponent)
Grover: O(√N) = O(2^{n/2}): quadratic speedup = EML-2 (halves exponent)

shor.reason: Shor: O(n³) quantum gates = EML-2 (polynomial in n = exp(3·ln n))
shor.implication: Shor reduces RSA (EML-∞ classical) to EML-2 (quantum polynomial)

shor.reason: Shor: O(n³) quantum gates = EML-2 (polynomial in n = exp(3·ln n))
shor.implication: Shor reduces RSA (EML-∞ classical) to EML-2 (quantum polynomial)

post_quantum.insight: Quantum computing moves RSA from EML-∞ to EML-2 — post-quantum crypto needs new EML-∞ problems

eml_depth_summary.EML-0: OTP cipher (perfect secrecy); Caesar/Vigenère; RSA modulus arithmetic given factors
eml_depth_summary.EML-1: Merkle-Damgård chain (iterated composition); Gaussian LWE noise per component
eml_depth_summary.EML-2: RSA/DH computation (modular exp); GNFS complexity; birthday bound; Shor/Grover; power-law key sizes
eml_depth_summary.EML-\u221e: Factoring n; discrete log; ECDLP; hash preimage/collision; LWE hardness (gap-SVP)

rabbit_hole_log: Cryptographic hardness = EML asymmetry: the same modular exponentiation that is EML-2 to compute forward is EML-∞ to invert backward. Cryptography exists precisely because EML depth is not preserved under inversion.

GNFS is exactly EML-2: the best classical factoring algorithm runs in exp((ln n)^{1/3}·(ln ln n)^{2/3}). This is two nested logarithms under an exponential = EML-2. RSA's security is that EML-2 classical effort is still enormous for 2048-bit keys.

Shor's algorithm is an EML-depth reduction: quantum computing changes RSA from EML-∞ (classical) to EML-2 (polynomial quantum). Post-quantum cryptography seeks functions that remain EML-∞ even for quantum computers. LWE achieves this via worst-case lattice hardness.

The hash function avalanche effect is EML-∞ by design: cryptographers engineer hash functions to have no EML-finite description of their input-output behavior. The EML-∞ property is the goal of hash design, not an accident.

connections.to_session_71: PRNGs are EML-2 (deterministic recurrence). Hash outputs target EML-∞ (true randomness). Crypto bridges them.
connections.to_session_69: Algorithmic randomness: hash outputs are computationally EML-∞ (pseudo-random oracle). Kolmogorov complexity analog.
